title: Merging Two Sorted Doubly Linked Lists in Reverse Order

statement.markdown: You are given the heads of two sorted doubly linked lists. Merge the linked lists in reverse order. The new merged linked list should be made by new node values which are the sum of corresponding nodes from the two input lists at each merge step. The result should also be a sorted doubly linked list in reverse order.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a doubly linked list head node.
statement.outputFormat: Return the head of the merged linked list.
statement.notes: Note that the new node values are the sum of corresponding nodes from the two input lists.

examples.explanation: The linked lists are merged in reverse order.

hints.text: Start with the last node of both lists and merge them into a new list.

editorial.title: Merging Two Sorted Doubly Linked Lists in Reverse Order
editorial.intuition: Merge two sorted doubly linked lists in reverse order by starting with the last node of both lists and merging them into a new list.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to merge the linked lists in reverse order by starting with the last node of both lists and merging them into a new list.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to merge two sorted doubly linked lists in reverse order by starting with the last node of both lists and merging them into a new list.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution is proved by showing that the linked lists are merged in reverse order.
editorial.dryRun: Here is an example of how to merge two sorted doubly linked lists in reverse order.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: Node* merge(Node* head1, Node* head2) { if (!head1) return head2; Node* merged = head1; Node* curr = head1; while (head2) { Node* next = head2->next; if (curr->val < head2->val) { curr->next = head2; curr = curr->next; } else { curr->next = merged; curr = curr->next->next; } head2 = next; } return merged; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public Node merge(Node head1, Node head2) { if (head1 == null) return head2; Node merged = head1; Node curr = head1; while (head2 != null) { Node next = head2.next; if (curr.val < head2.val) { curr.next = head2; curr = curr.next; } else { curr.next = merged; curr = curr.next.next; } head2 = next; } return merged; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def merge(self, head1, head2) -> Node: if not head1: return head2; merged = head1; curr = head1; while head2: next = head2.next; if curr.val < head2.val: curr.next = head2; curr = curr.next; else: curr.next = merged; curr = curr.next.next; head2 = next; return merged;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function merge(head1, head2) { if (!head1) return head2; var merged = head1; var curr = head1; while (head2) { var next = head2.next; if (curr.val < head2.val) { curr.next = head2; curr = curr.next; } else { curr.next = merged; curr = curr.next.next; } head2 = next; } return merged; }

functionSignature.python: Node merge(self, head1: Node, head2: Node) -> Node

solutions.python:
class Solution:
  def merge(self, head1: Node, head2: Node) -> Node:
    if not head1: return head2
    merged = head1
    curr = head1
    while head2:
      next = head2.next
      if curr.val < head2.val:
        curr.next = head2
        curr = curr.next
      else:
        curr.next = merged
        curr = curr.next.next
      head2 = next
    return merged

  def main(self):
    head1 = Node(1)
    head1.next = Node(3)
    head1.next.next = Node(9)
    head1.next.next.next = Node(5)
    head1.next.next.next.next = Node(2)
    head1.next.next.next.next.next = Node(4)
    head1.next.next.next.next.next.next = Node(7)
    head1.next.next.next.next.next.next.next = Node(6)

    head2 = Node(3)
    head2.next = Node(6)
    head2.next.next = Node(8)
    head2.next.next.next = Node(5)
    head2.next.next.next.next = Node(1)
    head2.next.next.next.next.next = Node(0)
    head2.next.next.next.next.next.next = Node(8)
    head2.next.next.next.next.next.next.next = Node(4)

    merged = self.merge(head1, head2)
    while merged:
      print(merged.val, end=' ')
      merged = merged.next
